scope_boundary: synthetic value-conflict record discipline and finite Lean value-conflict predicates only; no moral correctness, value-conflict classification quality, reviewer independence, human-review quality, tribunal quality, source interpretation, runtime policy behavior, or source-derived support-state promotion
transition_reason: The repository now has a value-conflict schema, a protocol fixture, finite ValueConflict Lean predicates, proof adequacy review text that keeps the target narrow, exact source mappings for the Moral Uncertainty chapter, and a synthetic value-conflict harness with valid and expected-invalid fixtures. Those artifacts make multi-axis classification, stakeholder and evidence requirements, high-stakes review routing, residual uncertainty preservation, authority narrowing, dissent payloads, bounded-decision revisit conditions, and deprecated-premise blocking executable at the record level. They still do not establish moral correctness, classification quality, reviewer independence, human-review quality, tribunal quality, source interpretation adequacy, or runtime policy behavior.
required_artifacts: reviewed value-conflict scenario library with positive and negative controls
classification-quality review across contested moral cases
human-review or tribunal-quality review for high-stakes conflict decisions
dissent preservation test over actual bounded decisions
runtime policy trace showing authority narrowing and revisit triggers under conflict
independent review of source interpretation adequacy
accepted upward evidence-transition review for any narrowed stronger claim
verification_command: python3 scripts/validate_value_conflicts.py && python3 scripts/validate_evidence_transitions.py && python3 scripts/validate_source_evidence_audit.py && python3 scripts/validate_v1_status_snapshot.py
negative_results: schema validation checks record shape rather than moral adequacy
finite Lean predicates remain useful but too narrow for moral-correctness or policy-quality claims
classification quality, human-review quality, tribunal quality, dissent preservation over real decisions, source interpretation review, and deployed runtime policy traces are not implemented
downgrade_triggers: chapter text interpreted as moral-correctness evidence
synthetic classification fixture interpreted as classification-quality evidence
review-route fixture interpreted as human-review or tribunal-quality evidence
bounded decision fixture treated as deployed runtime policy behavior
promotion_burden: Move above argument only after the claim is narrowed to the synthetic record discipline that passed, or after reviewed scenario libraries, classification-quality review, human-review or tribunal-quality review, dissent-preservation tests, runtime policy traces, and independent source-interpretation review are recorded with accepted evidence-transition records.
limitations: synthetic fixtures do not classify open-ended moral conflicts
schema validation does not prove moral correctness or stakeholder adequacy
finite Lean predicates do not implement a value-conflict policy engine
review-route text does not prove reviewer independence or review quality
bounded-decision fixtures do not prove authority narrowing in a deployed system
no independent moral, governance, or source-interpretation review has been recorded
reviewer_independence: local maintainer-agent review only; not an independent moral-philosophy, governance, reviewer-quality, policy-runtime, or source-interpretation audit
acceptance_blockers: no dissent-preservation test over real decisions
non_claims: does not promote Moral Uncertainty above argument
does not prove value-conflict classification quality
does not prove reviewer independence, human-review quality, or tribunal quality
does not prove runtime policy behavior
